mnemonics.title: JSP Implicit Objects
mnemonics.content: Real Rabbits Often Sing At Church, Preaching Peace Everyday (Request, Response, Out, Session, Application, Config, PageContext, Page, Exception)

mnemonics.title: Variable Declaration Steps
mnemonics.content: DI-ME: Declare → Initialize → Modify → Execute (Steps for proper variable handling)

mnemonics.title: JSP Scopes
mnemonics.content: PRSA: Page < Request < Session < Application (Scope visibility hierarchy)

mnemonics.title: Primitive Data Types
mnemonics.content: Be Safe, I Long For Delicious Chocolate Brownies (Byte, Short, Int, Long, Float, Double, Char, Boolean)

mnemonics.title: Servlet Lifecycle
mnemonics.content: ISD: Init() → Service() → Destroy() (Servlet phase sequence)

acronyms.usage: JSP scoping order from narrowest to broadest

acronyms.expansion: Client-Side Object Offering Key Information Exchange
acronyms.usage: Remember cookie purpose in session management

acronyms.expansion: Java Server Page Variables, Attributes, References
acronyms.usage: Three ways to store data in JSP

visualTips.visualization: Imagine TV remotes (references) pointing to actual TVs (objects) in a showroom
Russian nesting dolls - smallest (page), medium (request), large (session), biggest (application)
Browser as a cookie jar storing labeled treats (user data) with expiration dates
Toolbelt with 9 tools labeled request, response, session, etc. - always available in JSP
Empty boxes (declared variables) vs boxes with items (initialized variables)

analogies.analogy: Like hotel key cards - the card (reference) isn't the room (object), but gives access to it
Amusement park hand stamp - lets you re-enter (maintain state) throughout the day
Luggage tags - small pieces of info attached to travelers (users) between destinations
Office bulletin board - visible to everyone (all users) and persists until renovation (server restart)
